title: MetricComponents
summary: Records the metric and inverse-metric block components of a Poincaré normal-form metric.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Records the metric and inverse-metric block components of a Poincaré normal-form metric.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows Ambient/PE/MetricComponents.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Radial and tangential indices in Poincare normal form. Its constructors are: radial with arguments : PEIndex Iota; tangential with arguments : Iota maps to PEIndex Iota.
The Kronecker delta on PE normal-form indices.
correspondence.leanType: def kronecker [DecidableEq Iota] (I J : PEIndex Iota) : Real

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For I of type PEIndex Iota, kronecker I I equals 1.
correspondence.leanType: theorem kronecker_self [DecidableEq Iota] (I : PEIndex Iota) : kronecker I I = 1

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For i of type Iota, kronecker radial tangential i equals 0.
correspondence.leanType: theorem kronecker_radial_tangential [DecidableEq Iota] (i : Iota) : kronecker radial (tangential i) = 0

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For i of type Iota, kronecker tangential i radial equals 0.
correspondence.leanType: theorem kronecker_tangential_radial [DecidableEq Iota] (i : Iota) : kronecker (tangential i) radial = 0

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: PE indices are equivalent to an optional tangential index. In explicit terms, its defining expression is to Fun | radial maps to none | tangential i maps to some i inv Fun | none maps to radial | some i maps to tangential i left inv : equals by intro I cases I <;> rfl right inv : equals by intro I cases I <;> rfl.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{equivOption}=\operatorname{toFun} | \operatorname{radial} => \operatorname{none} | \operatorname{tangential} i => \operatorname{some} i \operatorname{invFun} | \operatorname{none} => \operatorname{radial} | \operatorname{some} i => \operatorname{tangential} i \operatorname{left\_inv} = \operatorname{by} \operatorname{intro} I \operatorname{cases} I <;> \operatorname{rfl} \operatorname{right\_inv} = \operatorname{by} \operatorname{intro} I \operatorname{cases} I <;> \operatorname{rfl}
correspondence.leanType: def equivOption : PEIndex Iota ≃ Option Iota

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Split a finite PE-index sum into radial and tangential parts. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,R\colon \mathrm{Type} w,\;\operatorname{Fintype} \operatorname{Iota},\;\operatorname{AddCommMonoid} R,\;f\colon \operatorname{PEIndex} \operatorname{Iota} \longrightarrow R,\quad (\sum I \colon \operatorname{PEIndex} \operatorname{Iota}, f I) = f \operatorname{radial} + \sum i \colon \operatorname{Iota}, f (\operatorname{tangential} i)
correspondence.leanType: theorem sum_index {R : Type w} [Fintype Iota] [AddCommMonoid R] (f : PEIndex Iota -> R) : (∑ I : PEIndex Iota, f I) = f radial + ∑ i : Iota, f (tangential i)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Boundary metric components and their formal inverse at a fixed radius. Its mathematical data are: r: Real; h: Iota maps to Iota maps to Real; h Inv: Iota maps to Iota maps to Real.

Covariant components of gPlus. In explicit terms, for c of type penormal metric components iota, its defining expression is | PEIndex.radial, PEIndex.radial maps to C.r to the power 2 ⁻¹ | PEIndex.radial, PEIndex.tangential maps to 0 | PEIndex.tangential, PEIndex.radial maps to 0 | PEIndex.tangential i, PEIndex.tangential j maps to C.r to the power 2 ⁻¹ times C.h i j.
correspondence.leanType: def gPlusDown (C : PENormalMetricComponents Iota) : PEIndex Iota -> PEIndex Iota -> Real

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Contravariant components of gPlus. In explicit terms, for c of type penormal metric components iota, its defining expression is | PEIndex.radial, PEIndex.radial maps to C.r to the power 2 | PEIndex.radial, PEIndex.tangential maps to 0 | PEIndex.tangential, PEIndex.radial maps to 0 | PEIndex.tangential i, PEIndex.tangential j maps to C.r to the power 2 times C.h Inv i j.
correspondence.leanType: def gPlusUp (C : PENormalMetricComponents Iota) : PEIndex Iota -> PEIndex Iota -> Real

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For C of type PENormal Metric Components Iota, g Plus Down C PEIndex.radial PEIndex.radial equals C.r to the power 2 ⁻¹.
correspondence.leanType: theorem gPlusDown_radial_radial (C : PENormalMetricComponents Iota) : gPlusDown C PEIndex.radial PEIndex.radial = (C.r ^ 2)⁻¹

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For C of type PENormal Metric Components Iota, and i, j of type Iota, g Plus Down C PEIndex.tangential i PEIndex.tangential j equals C.r to the power 2 ⁻¹ times C.h i j.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,C\colon \operatorname{PENormalMetricComponents} \operatorname{Iota},\;i,j\colon \operatorname{Iota},\quad \operatorname{gPlusDown} C (\operatorname{PEIndex}.\operatorname{tangential} i) (\operatorname{PEIndex}.\operatorname{tangential} j) = (r_{\mathrm{C}} ^ 2)⁻¹ \cdot h_{\mathrm{C}} i j
correspondence.leanType: theorem gPlusDown_tangential_tangential (C : PENormalMetricComponents Iota) (i j : Iota) : gPlusDown C (PEIndex.tangential i) (PEIndex.tangential j) = (C.r ^ 2)⁻¹ * C.h i j

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For C of type PENormal Metric Components Iota, g Plus Up C PEIndex.radial PEIndex.radial equals C.r to the power 2.
correspondence.leanType: theorem gPlusUp_radial_radial (C : PENormalMetricComponents Iota) : gPlusUp C PEIndex.radial PEIndex.radial = C.r ^ 2

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For C of type PENormal Metric Components Iota, and i, j of type Iota, g Plus Up C PEIndex.tangential i PEIndex.tangential j equals C.r to the power 2 times C.h Inv i j.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,C\colon \operatorname{PENormalMetricComponents} \operatorname{Iota},\;i,j\colon \operatorname{Iota},\quad \operatorname{gPlusUp} C (\operatorname{PEIndex}.\operatorname{tangential} i) (\operatorname{PEIndex}.\operatorname{tangential} j) = r_{\mathrm{C}} ^ 2 \cdot C.\operatorname{hInv} i j
correspondence.leanType: theorem gPlusUp_tangential_tangential (C : PENormalMetricComponents Iota) (i j : Iota) : gPlusUp C (PEIndex.tangential i) (PEIndex.tangential j) = C.r ^ 2 * C.hInv i j

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Left-inverse condition for the boundary metric block. In explicit terms, for c of type penormal metric components iota, its defining expression is for every i j : Iota, ∑ k : Iota, C.h Inv i k times C.h k j equals if i equals j then 1 else 0.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{BoundaryLeftInverse}(C)=\forall i j \colon \operatorname{Iota}, (\sum k \colon \operatorname{Iota}, C.\operatorname{hInv} i k \cdot h_{\mathrm{C}} k j) = \operatorname{if} i = j \operatorname{then} 1 \operatorname{else} 0
correspondence.leanType: def BoundaryLeftInverse [Fintype Iota] [DecidableEq Iota] (C : PENormalMetricComponents Iota) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The displayed contravariant components are a left inverse of the Poincare metric components. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,\operatorname{Fintype} \operatorname{Iota},\;\operatorname{DecidableEq} \operatorname{Iota},\;C\colon \operatorname{PENormalMetricComponents} \operatorname{Iota},\;hr\colon r_{\mathrm{C}} \ne 0,\;hbase\colon \operatorname{BoundaryLeftInverse} C,\;I,J\colon \operatorname{PEIndex} \operatorname{Iota},\quad (\sum K \colon \operatorname{PEIndex} \operatorname{Iota}, \operatorname{gPlusUp} C I K \cdot \operatorname{gPlusDown} C K J) = \operatorname{PEIndex}.\operatorname{kronecker} I J
correspondence.leanType: theorem gPlusUp_mul_gPlusDown [Fintype Iota] [DecidableEq Iota] (C : PENormalMetricComponents Iota) (hr : C.r ≠ 0) (hbase : BoundaryLeftInverse C) (I J : PEIndex Iota) : (∑ K : PEIndex Iota, gPlusUp C I K * gPlusDown C K J) = PEIndex.kronecker I J

references.note: Source for Poincaré normal form and its relation to straight ambient metrics.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
